revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24608848913|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:堂山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Dōyama Kofun Cluster |image = |location = {{ill|Daitō, Osaka|en|Daitō, Osaka|qid=Q386668|lt=Daitō, Osaka|1=Daitō, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan (Terakawa area) |coordinates = {{coord|34|42|50.2|N|135|38|51.7|E|display=inline|qq=}} |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |built = 5th to mid-7th century |map_size = 8 tumuli |historic_sites = City-Historic Site1 historic site (2012) |tangible_cultural_properties = Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property (2000) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Dōyama Kofun Cluster|coordinates=34|coordinates=42|coordinates=50.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=51.7|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|50.2|N|135|38|51.7|E|qq=}}|qq=}} }} '''Dōyama Kofun Cluster''' (堂山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Daitō, Osaka|en|Daitō, Osaka|qid=Q386668|lt=Daitō, Osaka|1=Daitō, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. The cluster has been Historic Site1 as a city historic site and dates back to the middleand late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. It was discovered during preliminary investigations for the construction of an Osaka Prefecture water distribution reservoir, and excavations have revealed multiple Kofuns from the 5th to mid-7th century. == Overview == === Discovery and Excavation === In January 1969, relics such as {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} and iron swords were found at the planned construction site for the Osaka Prefecture water reservoir at the summit of Dōyama Hill (elevation 98 meters). A preliminary survey revealed exposed fragments of iron swords and scattered haniwa fragments, suggesting the presence of a significant tumulus. Nearby, granite stones suspected to be the ceiling stones of a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} were observed, leading to speculation about additional tumuli in the area. Collectively referred to as the "Dōyama Kofun Cluster," the first discovered tumulus was Historic Site1 "Dōyama No. 1 Kofun." Excavations were conducted in February 1969 by the Osaka Prefectural and Daitō City Boards of Education, revealing details of the wooden coffin burial. A further survey of the mound was conducted in 1972. === No. 1 Kofun === Investigations revealed that Kofun No. 1 was constructed by shaping the granite bedrock at the hill’s summit, with little evidence of mound construction using soil. The mound, estimated to be a round tumulus 25 meters in diameter, lacked stone covering (''fukiishi''). The burial consisted of a composite box-shaped wooden coffin placed directly into a burial pit, with traces of a secondary coffin containing only grave goods. Relics included jade beads (''magatama''), jasper tubular beads, glass beads, and spindle whorls made of steatite. Additionally, 14 iron daggers were found north of the coffin and 11 to the south. The secondary coffin contained bronze armor (''tankō''), a horned helmet, iron swords, and agricultural tools. Haniwa relics included cylindrical and house-shaped haniwa, shields, and items interpreted as lids and quivers. These were mostly found in their original positions along the mound's edges. Early {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} fragments, including pedestal bowls and jars, were discovered at the southwestern base of the mound. Based on the triangular plate-laced techniques of the armor and the similarity of the Sue pottery to early examples from the Tōryō Kiln, the tumulus is dated to the early 5th century. The grave goods from Kofun No. 1 are Historic Site1 as Osaka Prefecture tangible cultural property. File:堂山1号墳出土 衝角付冑-2.JPG|Horned helmet (Osaka Prefecture designation1)
{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|en|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|qid=Q4557237|lt=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|1=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum}}.|qq=}} File:堂山1号墳出土 三角板革綴短甲-2.JPG|Triangular plate-laced armor (Osaka Prefecture designation1)
{{small|Exhibited at Chikatsu Asuka Museum.|qq=}} File:堂山1号墳出土 武器類.JPG|Weapons (Osaka Prefecture designation1)
{{small|Exhibited at Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum.|qq=}} File:堂山1号墳出土 農工具.JPG|Agricultural tools (Osaka Prefecture designation1)
{{small|Exhibited at Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum.|qq=}} === Tumuli No. 2–7 === Subsequent surveys identified six additional tumuli within the Dōyama Kofun Cluster. These later mounds, dating from the late 6th to mid-7th century, primarily feature {{ill|yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Relics such as ceramic coffins (''tōkan''), wooden coffin nails, and human bones were uncovered. The site was eventually preserved and developed as the Dōyama Kofun Cluster Historic Park, which opened in March 2012. File:堂山3号墳出土 陶棺.JPG|Tōkan from No. 3 Kofun
{{small|Exhibited at Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum.|qq=}} == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ''' burial goods from Kofun No. 1 - Preserved at Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. Designated February 8, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture designation1 List]. === Daitō City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Dōyama Kofun Cluster - Designated March 14, 2012[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture designation1 List]. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == * Katsumi Tashiro and Ken Segawa, ''Summary of Excavations at Dōyama Kofun Cluster,'' Osaka Prefectural Board of Education, 1973. == See Also == * {{ill|Uzumasa Kofun Cluster|ja|太秦古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzumasa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://ameblo.jp/moridukuri/entry-11202353884.html Dōyama Kofun Cluster Historic Park] (Daitō City Forest Preservation Club Blog) {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|堂山古墳群|version=84798076|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:堂山古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q108778886}} [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
